items.title: The National | Trump escalates threats after Iran rescue mission
items.summary_auto: April 5, 2026 | U.S. President Donald Trump fires off an expletive-laden ultimatum for Iran hours after a daring rescue mission to save an American pilot. The Artemis II crew gears up for a lunar flyby. And, advance polls open for three crucial federal byelections.

items.title: Trump's respect for King Charles possibly quashed desire to annex Canada, says royal commentator
items.summary_auto: An upcoming book authored by a prominent royal commentator says U.S. President Donald Trump was primarily interested in annexing Canadian territory just above the U.S. border — and his respect for King Charles may have quashed his interest.

items.title: WestJet introducing temporary fuel surcharge on companion voucher bookings
items.summary_auto: WestJet's new temporary fuel surcharge of $60 on all companion voucher bookings takes effect Wednesday, April 8.

items.title: Trophies, rivalries and a bar: What curlers expect in Rock League’s debut season
items.summary_auto: Curling is set to enter the great unknown. Debuting Monday, Rock League is just a little different from your typical tournament, from mixed 10-player teams to new rules to a bar that will be positioned in between the sheets on the ice.

items.title: Canada-Sweden curling feud reignites as Eriksson video stirs 'double-touch' debate
items.summary_auto: A video demonstration from Swedish curler Oskar Eriksson is adding fresh fuel to one of the most debated moments of Olympic curling.

items.title: N.S. premier blasts First Nation highway blockades, calls for RCMP to be respected
items.summary_auto: Nova Scotia Premier Tim Houston called the recent protests by First Nations over cannabis raids in their communities unacceptable in a statement posted to Facebook Saturday night. The protests blocked several highways throughout the province in reaction to RCMP seizing cannabis products from First Nations dispensaries.

items.title: On Easter, Pope Leo urges world leaders to end wars, conquest
items.summary_auto: Pope Leo urged global leaders in his Easter message on Sunday to end the conflicts raging across the world and abandon any schemes for power, conquest or domination.

items.title: Israeli airstrike kills 4 Palestinians in Gaza, medics say
items.summary_auto: An Israeli airstrike killed four Palestinians in the northern Gaza Strip on Sunday, local health authorities said, in the latest violence to overshadow ‌a fragile ceasefire amid a new push by mediators to bolster the agreement.

items.title: Rural municipalities call on Alberta government to open grizzly bear hunt
items.summary_auto: The Rural Municipalities of Alberta organization is advocating for the provincial government to legalize grizzly bear hunting in light of what Cardston County calls increased reports of negative human-bear interactions.

items.title: Yukon man grows pineapple at home — and it only took 9 years
items.summary_auto: When Alex Oberg finally cut into the fruit for a taste, it was a bit sour. 'We maybe could have waited a little bit longer,' he said.

items.title: Bogus maple syrup and WestJet under fire for flight cancellations: CBC's Marketplace Cheat Sheet
items.summary_auto: The CBC's Marketplace rounds up the consumer and health news you need from the week.

items.title: 5-year-old B.C. boy celebrates birthday at recycling centre
items.summary_auto: For most people, recycling is a chore. But for Himmat Rai, a five-year-old living in Maple Ridge, B.C., he says it's his "favourite thing to do." And to celebrate his passion for recycling, Rai and over a dozen of his classmates celebrated his fifth birthday at the local recycling centre.

items.title: Ontario supports House of Commons petition to allow top military honour to be awarded to late Ontario veteran
items.summary_auto: A House of Commons petition that calls on the federal government to establish an independent review board for military honours, such as the Victoria Cross, now has support from Ontario’s provincial government.

items.title: 'Avoid the honk': Interactive map helps Waterloo students stay away from Canada geese
items.summary_auto: A University of Waterloo student has launched a platform to help students navigate “aggressive geese” on campus.

items.title: Blinded by the headlights? Transport Canada wants to know
items.summary_auto: A London, Ont., optometrist says she's hearing more concerns from patients about  difficulty driving in the dark and headlight brightness. Transport Canada is asking drivers, pedestrians and cyclists to share their experiences with vehicle headlight glare through an online survey.

items.title: COVID-19 wreaked havoc on this N.S. seniors' group. It's now trying to rebuild
items.summary_auto: An organization that has advocated for Nova Scotia seniors for more than five decades says there's a sad reason why its membership numbers have dropped sharply in recent years. "We lost a lot of people through COVID," says the president of the Nova Scotia Federation of Seniors.

items.title: It stung to start over at the bottom of the career ladder when I moved to Canada. Now I help other newcomers
items.summary_auto: When Yemisi Peters moved from Nigeria to Edmonton, she was unsure how to navigate Canadian workplaces and faced many cultural shocks. Over time, she had to stop hiding who she was and her experience.

items.title: Kadin Savikataaq has been missing for a year and a half. Her family is still searching
items.summary_auto: On Aug. 19, 2024, 20-year-old Kadin Savikataaq went out for the night and never returned. The truck she'd been driving was discovered submerged in Hudson Bay, but a year and a half later, Kadin is still missing. RCMP say she likely drowned, but her family doesn't agree. They say they'll never stop looking for her.

items.title: Influencers tout injectable peptides as key to youth and beauty. Scientists say they're 'a giant scam'
items.summary_auto: Crocodile dung. Tapeworms. Raw meat facials. Yes, at some point in history these wildly popular — and wildly unproven — treatments were how people tried to stay young. Right now, plenty of influencers will tell you that injectable peptides are the answer. But the scientists? Not so much.

items.title: This 12-year-old Toronto-area golfer is representing Canada at the Augusta National
items.summary_auto: A twelve-year-old from Vaughan is competing for a coveted junior golfing title at Augusta National ahead of the Masters.

items.title: How Brantford, Ont., researchers are using VR gaming to help older adults with dementia stay fit
items.summary_auto: Researchers from Wilfrid Laurier University partnered with Brantford’s Adult Recreation Therapy Centre for a trial on a game aimed at supporting therapeutic movements and engaging users' brains — work aided by funding from the Alzheimer Society: Brant, Haldimand Norfolk, Hamilton Halton.

items.title: Amid political tensions, King Charles will be in a 'delicate diplomatic situation' during U.S. state visit
items.summary_auto: The timing of King Charles's upcoming state visit to the United States has sparked controversy and questions — particularly given the ongoing U.S.-Israel war against Iran, the geopolitical turmoil it has caused and the current strain in the relationship between the United Kingdom and the U.S.

items.title: Injectable peptides touted as new fountain of youth. But the science isn't there
items.summary_auto: Crocodile dung. Tapeworms. Raw meat facials. Yes, at some point in history these wildly popular — and wildly unproven — treatments were how people tried to stay young. Right now, plenty of influencers will tell you that injectable peptides are the answer. But the scientists? Not so much.

items.title: Ban on rescue dogs from countries at risk of spreading rabies to remain, CFIA says
items.summary_auto: The federal government says a ban on the import of rescue dogs into Canada will remain in place until further notice due to rabies concerns. Animal advocacy groups say the restrictions — which were brought in more than three years ago and originally meant to be temporary — are preventing healthy animals from legitimate international rescues from finding homes in Canada.

items.title: Project Hail Mary and Artemis II's launch reflect a long parallel between NASA and Hollywood
items.summary_auto: Artemis II's launch and Project Hail Mary's stunning success reflect a long line of films about space — films that similarly released alongside real life space-exploration projects, or increased public fascination with the subject.
